_meta.extracted_by: whole-word match over baked WEB · curated by claude-opus-4-8 · 2026-07-03
_meta.scope: Distinctive persons & places, each scoped to the books where its name is unambiguous (Torah + OT narrative & prophets).
_meta.limitations: Exact-name match only, no coreference or pronoun anaphora. Each entity is confined to a curated `books` scope, so recall is deliberately bounded (a figure named outside its scope gets no chip) in exchange for precision. Ambiguous common names the seed cannot safely disambiguate — most of the New Testament (John, Mary, Simon, James, Judas), tribe-vs-person eponyms outside the Torah — are LEFT for the frontier sweep over all 66 books (Session 2), not approximated here. A verified seed, not the sweep.
_meta.contested_policy: Where a tradition disputes an identity or reading, the entity carries a `contested` block with the competing views and their sources. The app never presents one lens as neutral fact.

entities.summary: The first man, formed from the dust of the ground.
The first woman, 'the mother of all living'.
First son of Adam and Eve; killed his brother Abel.
Second son of Adam; a keeper of sheep, slain by Cain.
Third named son of Adam, appointed 'in place of Abel'.
Walked with God; 'he was not, for God took him'.
Longest-lived man recorded in scripture.
Righteous in his generation; built the ark, survived the flood.
Son of Noah; ancestor of the Semitic peoples.
Son of Noah; father of Canaan.
Son of Noah.
Father of Abram, of Ur of the Chaldees.
The patriarch of faith; called from Ur, promised a nation.
Wife of Abraham; mother of Isaac in her old age.
Sarah's Egyptian handmaid; mother of Ishmael.
Son of Abraham and Hagar; father of twelve princes.
Abraham's nephew; dwelt in Sodom, delivered from its destruction.
King of Salem and priest of God Most High; blessed Abram.

contested.why: His origin and identity are read differently across traditions.

views.view: A historical Canaanite priest-king of Salem.
Identified with Shem, son of Noah.
A type of the eternal priesthood of Christ, 'without genealogy'.

entities.summary: The son of promise; offered on Moriah, father of Jacob and Esau.
Wife of Isaac; mother of Esau and Jacob.
Firstborn of Isaac; sold his birthright; father of Edom.
Younger son of Isaac, renamed Israel; father of the twelve tribes.
Beloved wife of Jacob; mother of Joseph and Benjamin.
Wife of Jacob; mother of six of the tribes incl. Judah and Levi.
Brother of Rebekah; father of Rachel and Leah.
Firstborn of Jacob by Leah.
Second son of Jacob by Leah.
Third son of Jacob; ancestor of the priestly tribe.
Fourth son of Jacob; ancestor of the royal and Messianic line.
Beloved son of Jacob; sold into Egypt, rose to be its ruler.
Youngest son of Jacob, Rachel's second son.
Younger son of Joseph, blessed above the firstborn.
Firstborn son of Joseph.
Egyptian officer who bought Joseph.
Drawn from the water; led Israel out of Egypt; received the Law.
Brother of Moses; first high priest of Israel.
Sister of Moses and Aaron; prophetess.
Priest of Midian; father-in-law of Moses.
Wife of Moses, daughter of Jethro.
Moses' minister; one of the two faithful spies; his successor.
The other faithful spy; 'he wholly followed the LORD'.
Led a rebellion against Moses and Aaron; swallowed by the earth.
The Mesopotamian seer hired to curse Israel, made to bless.
Son of Aaron; succeeded him as high priest.
Son of Eleazar; his zeal turned away the plague.
The Canaanite of Jericho who hid the spies and was spared.
Took of the devoted things at Jericho; brought defeat on Israel.
Prophetess and judge who summoned Barak against Sisera.
Captain who, with Deborah, routed Sisera's army.
Judge who broke Midian with three hundred men.
The Nazirite of prodigious strength; judge against the Philistines.
The woman of Sorek who coaxed out Samson's secret.
The Moabite who clung to Naomi and to her God; great-grandmother of David.
Widow of Bethlehem, mother-in-law of Ruth.
The kinsman-redeemer of Bethlehem who married Ruth.
Last of the judges; the prophet who anointed Saul and David.
The Bethlehemite, father of David.
First king of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin.
Saul's son; David's covenant friend.
Shepherd, psalmist and king; the man after God's own heart.
The Philistine champion of Gath, felled by David's sling.
Saul's captain of the host.
David's fierce and ruthless captain of the host.
David's son who rebelled and seized the kingdom for a season.
Wife of Uriah, then of David; mother of Solomon.
The prophet who confronted David and named Solomon.
David's son; builder of the Temple; the wise king.
First king of the northern ten tribes; set up the golden calves.
Solomon's son whose folly split the kingdom.
King of Israel who married Jezebel and served Baal.
Ahab's queen, patroness of Baal, foe of Elijah.
The Tishbite; called down fire on Carmel; taken up in a whirlwind.
Elijah's successor, who received a double portion of his spirit.
The furious charioteer anointed to end the house of Ahab.
King of Judah who trusted the LORD against Assyria.
The reforming king who restored the Book of the Law.
King of Babylon who razed Jerusalem and carried Judah away.
The exile in Babylon's court, keeper of visions, spared in the lions' den.
The scribe who brought the Law back to the returned exiles.
The cupbearer who rebuilt Jerusalem's wall.
The Jewish queen of Persia who saved her people.
Esther's guardian cousin who would not bow to Haman.
The Agagite whose plot against the Jews recoiled on himself.
The Persian king whose decree sent the exiles home.
The blameless man of Uz tried in the crucible of suffering.
The prophet of the Holy One of Israel and the Suffering Servant.

contested.why: The unity of the book of Isaiah is disputed.

views.view: One prophet, Isaiah of Jerusalem, wrote the whole book (8th c. BC).
Chapters 40–66 come from later hands ('Deutero-' and 'Trito-Isaiah').

entities.summary: The weeping prophet of Judah's last days.
Priest-prophet of the exile; seer of the chariot and the dry bones.
Prophet whose marriage figured God's love for faithless Israel.
The herdsman of Tekoa, prophet of justice.
The prophet who fled to Tarshish and preached to Nineveh.
Prophet who foretold the ruler to come from Bethlehem.
Prophet who learned that the just shall live by faith.
Post-exilic prophet of the coming King on a donkey.
